text: In Agile methodology, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on completing a set of tasks from a backlog, which is a prioritized list of work items. The team starts each sprint by selecting user stories—simple descriptions of features from the user's perspective. Daily standups are quick meetings where team members share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles they face. This process supports iterative development, meaning the product is improved gradually through repeated cycles. Feedback from each sprint helps the team adjust and deliver value more effectively.
